AIInteraction.title: AIInteraction
AIInteraction.description: Represents a single interaction with the AI text generation model, including the user's prompt and the AI's response.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AIInteraction entity.

promptText.description: The text prompt provided by the user to the AI model.

generatedText.description: The text response generated by the AI model.

modelUsed.description: The identifier of the AI model used for generation (e.g., 'gemini-2.0-flash').

timestamp.description: The date and time when the AI interaction occurred.

success.description: Indicates whether the AI generation was successful.

errorMessage.description: An optional error message if the AI generation failed.

definition.description: Represents a user's profile. This top-level document serves as the parent for all user-specific subcollections.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, corresponding to `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Stores individual AI text generation interactions for a specific user. Each document includes a denormalized 'ownerId' field (matching the '{userId}' path parameter) to ensure authorization independence and simple security rules. This allows for direct ownership checks without requiring 'get()' operations.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who performed this AI interaction, corresponding to `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: aiInteractionId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific AI interaction record.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure for the AI TextForge application centralizes user-specific AI interactions under a dedicated subcollection for each user. This design prioritizes **Authorization Independence** and **Queryability for Access Patterns (QAPs)** as core principles.

1.  **User Profiles (`/users/{userId}`):** A top-level collection `users` will contain basic user profile documents, implicitly representing the owner of nested data. Although the 'user' entity schema wasn't provided, this path serves as the anchor for all user-owned data.

2.  **User AI Interactions (`/users/{userId}/ai_interactions/{aiInteractionId}`):** Each user will have a `ai_interactions` subcollection, where every document represents a single `AIInteraction` (prompt and generated text).

    *   **Authorization Independence:** To achieve this, every `AIInteraction` document will include a denormalized `ownerId` field. This `ownerId` will directly correspond to the `{userId}` in the path. This allows Firestore Security Rules to evaluate authorization with a simple `request.auth.uid == resource.data.ownerId` check, completely avoiding `get()` calls to parent documents. This is critical for robust, atomic security and performance.

    *   **QAPs (Secure List Operations):** This hierarchical structure naturally supports QAPs. When a user requests to list their AI interactions, they will query `/users/{request.auth.uid}/ai_interactions`. The security rules can then easily ensure that `request.auth.uid` matches the `{userId}` wildcard in the path, guaranteeing that a user can only list their own interactions and cannot inadvertently (or maliciously) query another user's private data. This eliminates the need for complex, filtering-based rules that are insecure for `list` operations.

This design ensures that all documents within an `ai_interactions` subcollection share the same security posture (owner-only access), simplifying rule logic and enhancing debuggability.
